business_summary: Coca-Cola is the world's largest non-alcoholic beverage company. Its core, asset-light model is to manufacture and sell concentrates, syrups and beverage bases to a global network of independent bottling partners, who add water/sweetener, package, distribute and sell the finished drinks. Coca-Cola owns and markets the brands (Coca-Cola, Diet Coke, Sprite, Fanta, Minute Maid, Powerade, smartwater, Costa, fairlife, etc.) and collects high-margin concentrate revenue plus royalties, while the capital-heavy bottling sits largely off its own balance sheet. This concentrate model produces very high gross margins (~61%), durable pricing power, and steady cash flows across economic cycles. FY2024 net operating revenues were $47.1B.

moat.type: Brand strength + global distribution scale + bottler network with high switching costs; the Coca-Cola trademark is among the most valuable consumer brands on earth, giving durable pricing power.

valuation.method: pe (normalized comparable EPS ~$3.10 x 22/25/28x) + fcf_yield (normalized FCF/share ~$2.50 at 4.2/3.8/3.4%); blended

bull_case: Wide-moat, recession-resistant consumer staple: the Coca-Cola brand and global distribution give durable pricing power, shown by FY2024 organic revenue growth of 5% driven mostly by price/mix.
Asset-light concentrate model produces ~61% gross margins and strong, consistent free cash flow; capital intensity is low (FY2024 capex only $2.06B on $47.1B revenue).
Dividend aristocrat with 54 consecutive years of increases (~$2.04 paid in 2025, ~2.5% yield), backed by ample normalized free cash flow.
Strong near-term momentum: Q1 2026 net revenues +12% to $12.47B and comparable EPS +18% to $0.86, beating estimates; FY2026 comparable EPS growth guided to 8-9% on a ~$3 2025 base.
Steady ~10-15% ROIC and an enormous, diversified global volume base provide a long compounding runway in developing/emerging markets.
risks: Valuation: after a +17.85% six-month run, at $82.61 the price sits slightly above the top of our $63.86-$80.16 fair-value band (margin of safety roughly -15%); no cushion at today's price.
Health/regulatory pressure on sugary drinks (taxes, GLP-1 weight-loss drugs dampening consumption, labeling) could slow volume growth over time.
Currency headwinds: a large majority of revenue is international, so a strong US dollar can meaningfully cut reported and comparable results.
Net debt/EBITDA of ~2.86x is moderate but not low; rising rates raise interest cost (FY2024 interest expense $1.66B).

FY2024 GAAP results were distorted by $4.16B of other operating charges (incl. fairlife contingent consideration and impairments) and a ~$6B IRS tax-litigation deposit that depressed reported operating cash flow to $6.8B; the IRS dispute is a real contingent liability.
Slow-growth maturity: mid-single-digit revenue growth means returns depend heavily on price/mix, buybacks, and dividends rather than unit volume.
what_would_break_thesis: Gross margin falls below 55% for two consecutive quarters (erosion of pricing power / concentrate economics).
Net debt/EBITDA rises above 3.5x and stays there for more than two quarters.
Organic (currency-neutral) revenue declines year-over-year for two consecutive quarters, signaling structural volume loss rather than FX.
The dividend is cut or frozen (breaks the 54-year aristocrat record and signals cash-flow stress).
A serious accounting red flag or a materially adverse, large cash resolution of the IRS tax litigation that impairs the balance sheet.
Price runs far above fair value (e.g. >30% over our base case of ~$71.64) with no offsetting jump in normalized earnings power.
catalysts: Continued comparable EPS growth toward the FY2026 guided 8-9% (vs ~$3 in 2025).
Lower effective tax rate (guided ~19.9%) and favorable FX reversal boosting reported results.
Premium/portfolio expansion (fairlife, Costa, energy, water, zero-sugar) lifting price/mix.
Annual dividend increase (likely early 2027) extending the aristocrat streak.
Favorable resolution or de-risking of the IRS tax litigation.
